stories.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
stories.simple_summary: There are other web browsers you can use besides Chrome and Safari. The article lists some good alternatives available right now.
stories.why_it_matters: If your current browser is slow or you have privacy concerns, you could try a different one — but this is just information about choices, not something urgent you need to do.

stories.title: 3 Core Artificial Intelligence (AI) Market Leaders to Buy with $1,000 Right Now and Hold for the Next 20 Years
stories.simple_summary: This is an article suggesting which AI company stocks to buy if you have $1,000 to invest for 20 years.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on unless you're looking to buy stocks.

stories.title: We can debate the ethics of AI but can’t seem to change course | Letters
stories.simple_summary: An opinion piece about how people worry about AI ethics but society keeps moving forward with AI anyway.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a general discussion about concerns people have — not a specific change affecting your life right now.

stories.title: Gen Z isn’t happy about AI. Even when mom helped invent it.
stories.simple_summary: Younger people are unhappy about AI, including people whose parents work on creating it.
stories.why_it_matters: This is about how younger generations feel — interesting to know, but doesn't require any action from you.

stories.title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
stories.simple_summary: A company called Anthropic is creating a new AI tool to help scientists do research and discover new medicines faster.
stories.why_it_matters: This might lead to faster drug development down the road, though it's still early — this mainly affects scientists and medical researchers right now.

stories.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Midjourney’s medical scanner leaves many questions unanswered
stories.simple_summary: A company called Midjourney is working on a new AI-powered ultrasound scanner that doesn't use radiation. They showed a video of it but haven't proven it actually works yet.
stories.why_it_matters: This is very early stage and mostly a promise for now — nothing you can use today, and it's unclear if it will actually work as claimed.

stories.title: Argentina's plan for AI-run companies can't avoid humans
stories.simple_summary: Argentina is considering letting AI run some companies, but they're finding out that you still need humans involved.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a policy discussion in another country — it doesn't directly affect you unless you have business operations in Argentina.

stories.title: The Enterprise Gives AI Models a Path to Consumer Loyalty
stories.simple_summary: Businesses are using AI to help keep customers coming back and loyal to their brand.
stories.why_it_matters: If you own a small business, this shows it's worth learning how AI can help you understand and serve your customers better.

stories.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
stories.simple_summary: Mark Zuckerberg told his company that AI agents (smart computer programs that work on their own) aren't improving as fast as he expected.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors watching Meta — it means some of their goals are taking longer than planned, but doesn't change your daily life.

stories.title: Artificial intelligence: Yann LeCun works on more flexible AI
stories.simple_summary: A leading AI researcher is working on making AI more flexible and adaptable instead of so rigid.
stories.why_it_matters: This is technical research work — interesting for people who care about AI progress, but nothing you need to understand or act on right now.

stories.title: China’s Surreal AI-Generated Anti-Drug Ad Accidentally Made Drugs Look Cool
stories.simple_summary: China's government made an anti-drug advertisement using AI, but it accidentally made illegal drugs look appealing instead of dangerous.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a funny example of AI going wrong — it shows that AI-made content still needs humans to check it before releasing it.

stories.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
stories.simple_summary: A sandwich shop chain that's going public mentioned AI in their documents, even though AI has nothing to do with their business. This shows how much AI hype is overdone right now.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a wake-up call: companies use the word 'AI' to sound trendy even when it's not real or relevant — be skeptical when someone claims to use AI just because it sounds impressive.
